summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Games / criticism, Education / pedagogy. The strongest items in this run are Here's your next batch of Xbox Game Pass games for April, In Moves of the Diamond Hand, the vibes so far are transcendent, and If Windrose isn't quenching your sealord fantasies, maybe carving out your own Libertalia in pirate citybuilder Corsair Cove will.

mainItems.title: Here's your next batch of Xbox Game Pass games for April
mainItems.summary: Microsoft has revealed the next batch of games coming to its Xbox Game Pass subscription service this April, and into next month. Read more
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: In Moves of the Diamond Hand, the vibes so far are transcendent
mainItems.summary: Cosmo D's latest game Moves of the Diamond Hand is a direct sequel to Betrayal at Club Low, and while it's still in early access with a developing road map, the vibes so far are transcendent.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: If Windrose isn't quenching your sealord fantasies, maybe carving out your own Libertalia in pirate citybuilder Corsair Cove will
mainItems.summary: As if catching the same briny breeze as early access darling Windrose , unannounced but blatantly obvious Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced , and, uh, Soulframe , there’s yet more pirates coming in yet more pirate games.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Soulframe's latest major update is a broadside of pirate weapons, class abilities and outfits, plus falling alien skytowers
mainItems.summary: Pensive and flamboyant action-RPG Soulframe has just received a new update that adds a pirate class, together with rapiers, cutlasses, and a flouncy sailor's outfit.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Sekiro Jet Set Radio Mod adds skateboarding and graffiti to FromSoft's stylish ninja classic
mainItems.summary: The Dark Souls trilogy, Elden Ring , and Bloodborne remain the most celebrated and enduring FromSoftware games, yet the far more punishing and agile action of Sekiro shouldn't be forgotten, either.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
mainItems.summary: Vercel, a major development platform that hosts and deploys web apps, was compromised, and the hackers are attempting to sell stolen data.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
mainItems.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: A mine despoiled the beauty of the rainforest. This Goldman Prize winner took action
mainItems.summary: "We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.

mainItems.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
mainItems.summary: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

youtube.summary: The Smithsonian Zoo in Washington, D.C., says it will introduce a baby asian elephant calf to the public next Wednesday, in honor of Earth Day. The baby female, named Linh Mai, was born on Feb. 2.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
